column7: It's a book about zombies. Scary, haunted eyes. Maybe. A lot of text is blacked out, so it was tough to understand anything.

<style=prompt>"Perhaps the most dramatic example of parasitic brain control is the fungus Ophiocordyceps, which turns ants into "zombies" to ensure its own survival. A fungal spore lands on an ant, germinating and growing throughout its body, using the ant's own muscles to turn it into a puppet. As the infection reaches the brain, the fungus causes the ant to abandon its colony, climb to a specific height on a plant, and bite down on a leaf with a "fatal grip". Once in this optimal, humid position for fungal growth, the parasite kills the host and grows a spore-releasing stalk out of the ant's head to infect others."</style>
It's boring. It's one of Mom's books - she hasn't read it in a while. It's been on this shelf for ages… until it was taken away. And now it's back.
The faces on the cover are scribbled out, even the owl's. The writing inside is also mostly blacked out with marker. Reading it was hard.
It's a magnetic chess board… or a checkers board. It's also a box for the pieces when folded, but there are no pieces inside. We used to keep both kinds of pieces in here.
It had a pen once. Dad broke it against the wall a a long, long time ago.
The pages are glued together. It's not one of my sketchbooks, so it's okay.
It's an empty photo frame. It wasn't empty before. Why didn't they just scribble over the faces like on the books?
